name: Pallet Load (layer-fit method)
description: Whole-case pallet load estimate: whole cases per layer from the pallet footprint, whole layers from the usable stack height, and the resulting case count and volume utilisation. This is the conservative single-orientation estimate a load planner starts from.

inputs.name: Usable pallet length
inputs.description: Loadable deck length (48 in for a standard GMA pallet).

inputs.name: Usable pallet width
inputs.description: Loadable deck width (40 in for a standard GMA pallet).

inputs.name: Usable stack height
inputs.description: Maximum load height above the deck, after clearance and pallet-height allowances.

inputs.name: Case length
inputs.description: Outer case length; must be greater than zero.

inputs.name: Case width
inputs.description: Outer case width; must be greater than zero.

inputs.name: Case height
inputs.description: Outer case height; must be greater than zero.

outputs.name: Cases per layer
outputs.description: Whole cases that fit one layer in a single orientation.

outputs.name: Layers
outputs.description: Whole layers that fit within the usable stack height.

outputs.name: Volume utilisation
outputs.description: Share of the usable pallet cube occupied by the loaded cases.

expression: CPL = floor(PL / CL) * floor(PW / CW); NL = floor(PH / CH); cases = CPL * NL; util_pct = 100 * cases * (CL * CW * CH) / (PL * PW * PH)
assumptions: All cases are identical and loaded in a single orientation (case length along pallet length).
Cases stack in full columns; no interlocking, pinwheel, or mixed-layer patterns.
Cases stay within the pallet footprint — no overhang.
limitations: Single-orientation column stacking is a lower bound: rotating cases or mixing patterns within a layer often fits more (compare the cube-method upper bound in freight.pallet.cube_ratio).
Ignores weight limits — pallet, racking, and vehicle weight caps can bind before the cube does.
Ignores crush strength, load stability, and overhang rules, all of which constrain real stacking heights.

examples.note: 48 x 40 x 60 in usable pallet, 16 x 12 x 10 in cases: floor(48/16) x floor(40/12) = 3 x 3 = 9 cases per layer; floor(60/10) = 6 layers; 54 cases; utilisation = 100 x 54 x 1,920 / 115,200 = 90%.

citations.quote: Cube utilization. All stocking equipment should be filled to the maximum cube capacity ... Honeycombing is the percentage of storage space lost because of ineffective use of the cube capacity of a storage area.
